step_by_step_thinking: \boxed{A} Yes, treatment with Bacillus Calmette-Guérin (BCG) is commonly used for non-invasive forms of bladder cancer, especially for high-grade tumors such as carcinomas in situ (CIS) and high-grade T1 tumors. Multiple sources confirm BCG’s role as first-line therapy for high-risk non-muscle-invasive bladder cancer, demonstrating effectiveness in delaying or preventing progression through immunologic stimulation and direct toxicity to tumor cells.
